protocol.name: planning-gate
protocol.objective: Present a structured execution plan to the user before any workflow step runs. Collect user approval or abort. Return the runtime interaction mode (normal or YOLO) that the engine should use after approval.

mandates.mandate: Parse instructions.xml into a structured plan — do not execute any step
Present the plan in fixed section order for predictability
Wait for user selection: a (normal) | y (YOLO) | r (revise) | x (abort)
Never auto-approve. The user must explicitly select a or y.

plan-sections.section: High-level goal statement from the workflow's description, the owner agent, the declared inputs, and the expected outputs
Every file path the plan will write to, extracted from step template-output declarations
One line per step: step number, title, what the step produces, which skills it JIT-loads
The strict numerical step order as declared in instructions.xml
Which gates fire pre-start and post-complete, which cross-cutting disciplines are invoked

step.title: Parse instructions for plan content
step.action: Extract each step's number, title, actions, template-output (if any), skill references
Build the five Plan Sections listed above

step.title: Present plan
step.action: Render the plan sections to the user in fixed order: Context, Files to Modify, Detailed Edits, Implementation Order, Verification
Omit any section that is empty (e.g., a workflow with no template-outputs has no Files to Modify)

step.title: Collect user decision
step.action: Display prompt: "[a] Approve (normal) | [y] Approve (YOLO) | [r] Revise plan | [x] Abort"

step.title: Handle decision
step.action: Return runtime_mode = normal. Engine proceeds to step 9 of workflow flow.
Return runtime_mode = YOLO. Engine proceeds to step 9 of workflow flow.
Ask user for feedback on the plan. Regenerate plan incorporating feedback. Re-present via step 2.
HALT the workflow with status halted_user_abort. Write checkpoint with abort reason.
